title: Conflict-Driven Health Service Disruption Measurement In Fragile States
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring health service disruption caused by armed conflict, linking conflict-event data, health-facility data, and service-utilization data in fragile and conflict-affected states.
why_it_matters: Over 2 billion people live in conflict-affected countries. Health facilities are attacked, supply chains break, and health workers flee. WHO tracks attacks on healthcare but facility-level service disruption data is fragmentary. Connecting conflict-event databases like ACLED to health-service utilization data would enable the humanitarian sector to respond to disruption, not just count attacks.

success_metrics.target: Every disruption estimate links conflict-event, facility, and utilization data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
